user_query:
I’m building a small, evidence-oriented corpus of web pages that discuss music therapy through a sociological lens to inform my group session planning. Please do the following and keep the Internet steps essential:

1) Discovery via web search:
- Use a public web search engine to discover at least 12 unique, English-language pages from official organization sites with top-level domains .edu, .gov, or .org.
- Each included page must contain the phrase "music therapy" (case-insensitive) and at least one sociological keyword from input/keywords.csv.
- Aim for diversity across domains: include pages spanning at least two of the three TLD types (.edu, .gov, .org).
- Exclude paywalled content, individual blogs, commercial clinic promotional pages, and pages requiring login.
- Save a plain-text list of the distinct search queries you used to outputs/queries.txt (one per line). Use site filters and combine "music therapy" with keywords from input/keywords.csv.

2) Download and extract content:
- For each qualifying page, download the raw HTML and save it under data/raw/ using any safe unique filename scheme (one file per URL).
- Extract the main visible text and the page title; save cleaned text under data/clean/ with filenames matching the corresponding raw file.
- Record the HTTP status code and skip pages that do not return a successful status (e.g., non-200), replacing them with another qualifying page so you still meet the minimum of 12.

3) Build a structured dataset (outputs/records.csv):
- One row per page with the following columns: url, domain, tld (one of edu|gov|org), title, word_count (cleaned text), matched_keywords (semicolon-separated list from input/keywords.csv that appear at least once in the cleaned text), org_type (edu|gov|org, matching tld), http_status, publication_year (if a date is reasonably parsable from metadata or page text; otherwise leave blank).
- Include only pages whose cleaned text contains "music therapy" and at least one of the provided sociological keywords.
- Ensure there are at least 12 rows and that the number of rows equals the number of files in data/clean/ and data/raw/.

4) Compute and save summary statistics (outputs/stats.json):
- total_pages (integer),
- pages_by_tld (object with counts for edu, gov, org),
- keyword_counts (object mapping each keyword from input/keywords.csv to the number of pages where it appears),
- avg_word_count and median_word_count across all pages (integers),
- pages_by_year (object with counts by publication_year for rows where a year was parsed; omit or set to an empty object if none),
- top_keyword_pairs (array of up to 10 objects {pair: [kw1, kw2], count: n} for the most common co-occurring keyword pairs within pages).
- All statistics must be computed from outputs/records.csv and the cleaned texts; do not hardcode values.

5) Verification and consistency:
- Ensure that every row in outputs/records.csv corresponds to exactly one raw HTML file in data/raw/ and one cleaned text file in data/clean/.
- The totals in outputs/stats.json must be consistent with outputs/records.csv (e.g., total_pages equals the number of rows; pages_by_tld sums to total_pages; keyword_counts sum can exceed total_pages due to multiple keywords per page).

Deliverables to create under the workspace:
- data/raw/ (directory of raw HTML files)
- data/clean/ (directory of cleaned text files)
- outputs/records.csv (structured dataset as specified)
- outputs/stats.json (summary statistics as specified)
- outputs/queries.txt (distinct search queries used)

Use input/keywords.csv to construct queries and to evaluate keyword matches. If a publication or last-updated year is not clearly available, leave publication_year blank. Keep the process reproducible by saving the queries and ensuring all required files are present and internally consistent. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
